user_query: Process the March 2026 expense records and produce a submission-ready claim package. Read inputs from input/config.json and input/expenses.json. Then: 1) Normalize every expense to the schema {item_id, date, merchant, amount, currency, category, payment_method, business_purpose, project_or_client (optional), receipt_status, policy_risk, claim_status, block_reason (if blocked)}. 2) Apply these block rules exactly: - If receipt_status is missing or unreadable → claim_status=blocked with block_reason "missing receipt" or "unreadable receipt" respectively. - If business_purpose is empty/missing → claim_status=blocked with block_reason "missing business purpose". - Suspected duplicate detection is strict: for any collision on the same date + merchant + amount, block all collided items with block_reason "suspected duplicate". 3) No policy rules are provided; set policy_risk="policy not provided" for every item. 4) Compute totals by currency: - Ready section: total_claim_ready by currency, plus category totals by currency for ready items. - Blocked section: total_blocked by currency and blocked_count. Also compute missing_doc_count across all items where receipt_status ∈ {missing, unreadable}. 5) Create a concise, submission-ready text pack with exactly three sections labeled in this order: "Ready:", "Blocked:", and "Next action:". - Ready: list only claim-ready items, each line including the key fields and explicitly showing claim_status=ready and policy_risk. - Blocked: list only blocked items, each line including the key fields, claim_status=blocked, policy_risk, and the exact block_reason. - Include the required totals and missing_doc_count in their respective sections. - Next action: include a claim summary block that states the period from input/config.json, totals, item counts, and a short per-item note telling what to fix for each blocked item. 6) Write the human-readable pack to output/claim_pack.txt formatted as plain text. Also write a machine-readable normalized JSON array of all items (with the fields and computed statuses) to output/normalized.json. Amounts in the text file should be formatted with two decimals. Use the currency codes as provided in the items. Do not include any other files or outputs.
